channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania Plans to Boost Coal Use to Support Manufacturing Growth
item.description: Dodoma – Tanzania dey push hard for industrial growth, and dis one go sharply increase demand for coal. Mining Minister Anthony Mavunde talk say coal go play central role for powering local manufactur...

item.title: Kuwait Confirms Uninterrupted Supply of Diesel and Jet Fuel to Pakistan Amid Strait of Hormuz Closure
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Kuwait has formally assured Pakistan of full facilitation in the supply of diesel and jet fuel, which had been disrupted due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz amid the ongoing US-Israe...

item.title: Understanding Why You Feel Pressured Even When Everything Seems Fine
item.description: Sometimes we feel pressured or stressed even when nothing is actively wrong around us. This happens because our mind keeps juggling a lot of small unfinished tasks at the same time. Even simple things...

item.title: Rights Activist Alleges Police Abduction and Extortion in Anambra State
item.description: Na serious wahala dey happen for Anambra State as rights activist, Harrison Gwamnishu, don raise alarm say some police officers from the Anti-Cultism Unit for Enugu-Ukwu dey abduct, beat, and force pe...

item.title: Parliament Discusses CAPF Bill and Insolvency Code Amendments During Budget Session
item.description: On 30th March 2026, the Indian Parliament was engaged in deliberations over two significant legislative matters during the second part of the Budget session. The Lok Sabha passed the Insolvency and Ba...

item.title: Albanese calls for clarity on US objectives in Iran as Trump signals escalation and possible resource seizure
item.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has called for clearer direction from United States President Donald Trump regarding the objectives of the ongoing conflict in Iran, urging de-escalation ami...

item.title: Don Farrell advances discussions on increasing ACT Senate representation
item.description: Federal Labor senator and Special Minister of State Don Farrell has indicated that the ACT may soon gain extra Senate representation, describing the expansion of Parliament as characteristic of 'great...

item.title: BBC ends contract with Scott Mills following allegations about personal conduct
item.description: Scott Mills has been dismissed from the BBC following allegations regarding his personal conduct. The BBC confirmed that he is no longer contracted to work with the organisation, though it did not pro...

item.title: Ben&Ben gives early listen to reimagined 'Lifetime' ahead of release
item.description: OPM band Ben&Ben has offered fans a sneak peek of their reimagined track 'Lifetime' just hours before its official release. Known for their heartfelt lyrics and melodious compositions, the band has be...

item.title: Punjab Government Announces Schools Will Reopen from April 1 with Five-Day Week
item.description: Punjab Education Minister Rana Sikandar Hayat announced that schools across the province will reopen starting from April 1, 2026. Earlier, all educational institutions were closed from March 10 to 31 ...

item.title: Temporary Fuel Excise Cut Provides Short-Term Relief but Risks Fiscal Strain
item.description: The federal government has announced a three-month halving of the fuel excise, reducing costs by 26.3 cents per litre and temporarily removing the 32.4-cent heavy vehicle road user charge. Treasurer J...

item.title: Aiko Melendez’s Daughter Starts Acting Career with Short Film Role
item.description: The daughter of renowned Filipino actress Aiko Melendez has officially stepped into the entertainment scene with her acting debut in a short film. This marks a significant milestone for the young tale...

item.title: The Short-Lived Libertarian Experiment of Minerva Reefs
item.description: In the early 1970s, American real estate magnate Michael Oliver attempted to create a libertarian utopia by claiming the remote Minerva Reefs in the South Pacific. Inspired by the counterculture era a...

item.title: President Zardari and PM Shehbaz review Pakistan’s security and regional challenges
item.description: On Monday, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif met President Asif Ali Zardari to review Pakistan’s national security situation in the context of the ongoing war in Iran. The meeting followed a quadrilateral...

item.title: Global Oil Prices Soar Amid Rising Tensions Between US and Iran
item.description: The wahala for Middle East don make oil price jaga-jaga as it hit $117 per barrel. This na because US dey think say e fit carry out ground attacks against Iran’s Gulf islands, while Israel and Iran st...

item.title: Trump Urges Iran to Halt Uranium Enrichment Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: In a recent statement covered by The Wall Street Journal, former U.S. President Donald Trump declared that Iran would need to surrender its uranium as part of any future agreement aimed at reducing te...

item.title: Exmouth continues recovery after Cyclone Narelle
item.description: Exmouth, a well-known tourist town in Western Australia, is facing significant challenges after being struck by Cyclone Narelle over the weekend. Local authorities and residents are working tirelessly...

item.title: Deputy PM Ishaq Dar to visit China amid Pakistan's efforts for regional diplomacy
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar is scheduled to travel to China on March 31 following an invitation from Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi. This visit comes as Pakistan ...

item.title: Kiren Rijiju clarifies FCRA amendment debate ahead of Kerala Assembly elections
item.description: Ahead of the Kerala Assembly elections, Union Minister Kiren Rijiju has responded strongly to allegations from the Congress and Left parties, who labelled the recent amendment to the Foreign Contribut...

item.title: Manitoba refuses name-change request for trans refugee due to ID requirements
item.description: Martha Hozha, a 25-year-old Ukrainian refugee in Manitoba, has been unable to change her legal name to reflect her gender because the provincial government does not accept her refugee protection ID. A...
